mergeStrategy: Direct commits to main. Each FEAT task is a clean atomic commit; CODE-REVIEW-1, REFACTOR-REVIEW-1, MILESTONE-1, INT-001, REFACTOR-REVIEW-3, VERIFY-001, MILESTONE-FINAL gate progress. No merge step needed since work is on main.
description: Refactor P1 of approved 4-phase plan: introduce a single crate-wide TestDb test fixture (src/db/test_utils.rs) and migrate ~40 hand-rolled TempDir+open_connection+create_schema+run_migrations blocks across src/commands/, src/loop_engine/, src/db/, and src/learnings/. Deletes both legacy helpers (src/learnings/test_helpers.rs::setup_db and src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs::setup_test_db) while preserving every other symbol they export. Pure refactor — no production behavior change.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Approach before code. Anticipate edge cases. Consider 2-3 approaches, pick the best

2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — If a more sophisticated solution costs ~1 day now but saves ~2+ weeks post-launch, take that trade-off (1:10 ratio or better). We are pre-launch; foundations compound enormously
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable code, wired in according to the plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Self-critique after code. Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Quality dimensions explicit. Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — read qualityDimensions on every task; know what 'good' looks like before coding
Approach before code — consider 2-3 approaches with tradeoffs, pick the best, then implement
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations; if ~1 day now saves ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio), take the sophisticated path
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test; boundaries are where bugs hide
Self-critique after code — review your own implementation for correctness, style, and performance before moving on
Ship solid working code with tests to prove it
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code (test setup MAY use .expect() with diagnostic messages)

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Skipping or weakening any pre-existing test to make migration easier
Silently converting a partial-migration test (which calls migrate_up directly) into a fully-migrated test — would mask version-specific bugs
Exposing TestDb / setup_test_db to release builds — must be #[cfg(test)] gated

globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks in this PRD
criteria: Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test` (test count must equal pre-refactor count)
No skipped, ignored (#[ignore]), or weakened tests

All sweep tasks: `git diff` shows ONLY mechanical replacements of the 4-line setup pattern with `let db = setup_test_db();` (or unmigrated variant) and the corresponding rebinding of `&db.conn` / `&mut db.conn` / `db.db_dir()` at use sites — no logic changes
Helper module is `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate) mod test_utils;` so it contributes zero bytes to release binary

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

userStories.title: Initial tests for TestDb and setup_test_db helpers
userStories.description: Write unit tests for the new TestDb struct and both setup helpers BEFORE the helper module is created. Tests must distinguish 'fully migrated' from 'schema-only' state, and ensure the tempdir actually outlives the connection.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: `setup_test_db()` returns a TestDb whose conn reports schema_version == CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION (verifies all migrations applied)
Test: `setup_test_db_unmigrated()` returns a TestDb whose conn reports schema_version < CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION when any migrations exist (verifies migrations NOT applied)
Test: `TestDb::db_dir()` returns the same Path the tempdir was created at, and the path exists on disk while TestDb is alive
Test: dropping TestDb removes the tempdir from disk (assert directory exists before drop, gone after) — verifies RAII cleanup

Known-bad discriminator: at least one test would PASS for a stub helper that returned a fresh in-memory `Connection::open_in_memory()` but FAIL for the real helper (e.g., asserts the file at db_dir() exists)
Tests live in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block at the bottom of `src/db/test_utils.rs`
All tests use `assert_eq!` / `assert!` with descriptive messages; no `assert!(true)` or empty bodies
userStories.notes: TDD: write these tests first. They will fail to compile until FEAT-001 creates the module — that's expected. Use #[ignore] with a clear TODO comment if you want to commit the tests separately from the implementation, OR commit them together with FEAT-001. Reference: existing setup_db in src/learnings/test_helpers.rs:9-15 is the proven pattern; src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs:69-75 has the second copy. Learning [55] explicitly endorses 'single shared setup' as this codebase's preferred pattern.

correctness: Tests assert schema_version, NOT just 'no panic' — must distinguish fully migrated from schema-only
Tests verify TempDir lifetime is tied to TestDb (drop semantics)
Test for db_dir() must use a real filesystem assertion, not just type-check
style: Use `.expect("test DB setup: <step>")` not `.unwrap()` so failures point to test_utils.rs
Tests use `let db = setup_test_db();` style — no tuple destructuring

edgeCases: TempDir dropped before connection is closed: must be impossible by ownership (TestDb owns both)
Schema-only DB asked for migrated state: caller used the wrong helper — verify schema_version reflects choice
Two consecutive `setup_test_db()` calls return distinct TempDirs (no shared state)
Migration tests calling `migrate_up` after `setup_test_db_unmigrated()` arrive at the same state as `setup_test_db()` (helpers are equivalent up to migration application)
invariants: TestDb owns BOTH `conn: Connection` and `tmp: TempDir`; neither field can outlive the other
`setup_test_db_unmigrated()` returns a connection at the schema's bootstrap version (whatever `create_schema` produces, before migrations)
`db.db_dir()` is `&Path` borrowed from `db.tmp.path()` — never returns a fresh allocation

failureModes.cause: Tempdir creation fails (out of disk, permissions)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Helper panics via `.expect("test DB setup: create tempdir")` — diagnostic message points to test_utils.rs

failureModes.cause: Schema creation fails on existing migration
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Helper panics via `.expect("test DB setup: create schema")` — diagnostic message points to test_utils.rs

userStories.title: Foundation: create src/db/test_utils.rs with TestDb + setup_test_db + setup_test_db_unmigrated
userStories.description: Create the new test fixture module at src/db/test_utils.rs containing the TestDb struct and both helper functions. Wire it into src/db/mod.rs behind #[cfg(test)] pub(crate). This is the foundation — every later sweep task depends on it.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `src/db/test_utils.rs` exists with: `pub struct TestDb { pub conn: Connection, pub tmp: TempDir }`, `impl TestDb { pub fn db_dir(&self) -> &Path }`, `pub fn setup_test_db() -> TestDb`, `pub fn setup_test_db_unmigrated() -> TestDb`
Helpers use `.expect("test DB setup: <step>")` with distinct messages per step (tempdir, open, schema, migrations) — NOT `.unwrap()`
`src/db/mod.rs` adds `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate) mod test_utils;`
All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass

`cargo build --release` shows no test_utils symbols in the binary (verify with `cargo build --release && nm target/release/task-mgr 2>&1 | grep -c test_utils` returns 0, OR document why this can't be checked locally)
Module body is < 60 lines including doc comments and the test mod
userStories.notes: Foundation task — uses opus because architectural decisions here propagate to ~40 call sites. Imports: `use crate::db::{create_schema, migrations::run_migrations, open_connection};` and `use rusqlite::Connection; use std::path::Path; use tempfile::TempDir;`. Mirror src/learnings/test_helpers.rs:9-15 verbatim for the body (literally the same 4 calls), but wrap in `TestDb { conn, tmp }` instead of returning a tuple. Learning [55] (high confidence, 10/19 applied) endorses this consolidation. Reference src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs lines 69-75 to see the existing duplicated copy.

correctness: Helper bodies are functionally identical to existing 4-line pattern — same pragmas, same schema, same migration order
Both helpers return `TestDb` (not `Result<TestDb, _>`) — tests are allowed to panic on setup failure
Module gated by `#[cfg(test)]` so it contributes zero bytes to release binary
performance: No fixture caching, no shared state — each call gets a fresh tempdir + connection
Helper itself adds no measurable overhead vs the inlined 4-liner
style: Use `.expect(msg)` not `.unwrap()`; messages start with 'test DB setup:'
`pub` fields on TestDb (not getters) — call sites write `&db.conn`, `&mut db.conn` directly
Doc comments on the struct and both functions explaining when to use which
Follow existing import organization in nearby modules

userStories.title: Migrate src/learnings/ test sites + delete legacy setup_db
userStories.description: Replace 4-line setup blocks in src/learnings/ tests with the new helper. After all 5 callers are migrated, delete `setup_db()` from src/learnings/test_helpers.rs (lines 9-15). Preserve `retire_learning` and `insert_task_with_files` in that file.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of these files imports `crate::db::test_utils::setup_test_db` and uses `let db = setup_test_db();`: src/learnings/bandit.rs, src/learnings/crud/tests.rs, src/learnings/embeddings/mod.rs, src/learnings/recall/tests.rs, src/learnings/retrieval/tests.rs
src/learnings/test_helpers.rs::setup_db (lines 9-15) is DELETED
src/learnings/test_helpers.rs still exports retire_learning and insert_task_with_files (no other deletions)
All learnings/ tests pass: `cargo test --lib learnings`
No `temp_dir.path()` / `TempDir::new()` references remain in any of the 5 migrated files (use `db.db_dir()` and `setup_test_db()` instead)
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean
userStories.notes: Mechanical sweep. Pattern: replace `let temp_dir = TempDir::new()...; let mut conn = open_connection(temp_dir.path())...; create_schema(&conn)...; run_migrations(&mut conn)...;` with `let db = setup_test_db();`. Then update use sites: `&conn` → `&db.conn`, `&mut conn` → `&mut db.conn`, `temp_dir.path()` → `db.db_dir()`. Note that some callers use `let mut conn` for transactions — `db.conn` is a field, so write `&mut db.conn` for those uses. Existing local helpers in learnings/test_helpers.rs (`retire_learning`, `insert_task_with_files`) are KEPT.

correctness: Test count before and after migration is identical (no skipped/disabled tests)
Borrow-checker handling: `&mut db.conn` works for transaction sites that previously had `&mut conn`
Imports are minimal: `use crate::db::test_utils::setup_test_db;` (do NOT re-import open_connection / create_schema / run_migrations)
performance: Test runtime within ±10% of baseline (`cargo test --lib learnings`)
style: No leftover unused imports (`open_connection`, `create_schema`, `run_migrations`, `TempDir`)
`use rusqlite::Connection;` removed if no longer used in test module
Imports grouped per existing file conventions

userStories.title: Migrate src/loop_engine/ batch A (5 files)
userStories.description: Migrate the first batch of loop_engine test files from `crate::loop_engine::test_utils::setup_test_db` to `crate::db::test_utils::setup_test_db`. This batch covers heavy-hitter files. Old helper STAYS for now (deleted in FEAT-003c).
acceptanceCriteria: Each file imports `crate::db::test_utils::setup_test_db` (was `crate::loop_engine::test_utils::setup_test_db`) and uses `let db = setup_test_db();` style
Tuple destructuring `let (tmp, conn) = setup_test_db();` is fully replaced; subsequent uses become `&db.conn`, `&mut db.conn`, `db.db_dir()` as appropriate
Other imports from `loop_engine::test_utils` (e.g., `EnvGuard`, `CLAUDE_BINARY_MUTEX`, `insert_test_learning`, `setup_git_repo`) are PRESERVED — only `setup_test_db` import path changes
`cargo test --lib loop_engine::archive`, `loop_engine::batch`, `loop_engine::context`, `loop_engine::deadline`, `loop_engine::engine` all pass
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean
userStories.notes: loop_engine/ tests already use the EXISTING `setup_test_db` from `crate::loop_engine::test_utils`. The migration is two parts: (1) change the import path, (2) rebind the return type from `(TempDir, Connection)` to `TestDb` — `let (tmp, conn) = setup_test_db();` becomes `let db = setup_test_db();`. Many call sites bind `let mut conn` for transactions; these become `&mut db.conn`. Watch for lines like `tmp.path()` → `db.db_dir()`. Files in this batch: archive.rs, batch.rs, context.rs, deadline.rs, engine.rs.

correctness: Test count unchanged
Borrow checker: every `&mut conn` use site becomes `&mut db.conn` — verify no aliasing violations introduced
Other helpers from loop_engine::test_utils are untouched (verify by re-running affected tests)
style: Imports adjusted minimally (only setup_test_db path changes)
Variable name conventions preserved (tests that named the binding `temp_dir` may rename to `db` — preferred — or stay descriptive)

userStories.title: Migrate src/db/migrations/ test sites with care for stepwise tests (6 files)
userStories.description: Migrate src/db/migrations/ test sites. CRITICAL: tests that explicitly call `migrate_up` to step through versions need `setup_test_db_unmigrated()` (NOT `setup_test_db()`) — converting them to the fully-migrated helper would silently destroy version-stepping coverage. Tests that only assert the final fully-migrated state can use `setup_test_db()`.
acceptanceCriteria: Each file's tests migrated to the appropriate helper variant
Tests that call `migrate_up`/`migrate_down` directly use `setup_test_db_unmigrated()` so the migration call is meaningful (not a no-op)
Tests that only assert the post-migration steady state use `setup_test_db()`

Each test that calls `migrate_up()` after `setup_test_db_unmigrated()` produces the same final schema_version as the previous test — verify with at least one before/after comparison test that proves equivalence
All migration tests pass: `cargo test --lib db::migrations`
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean
userStories.notes: Files: src/db/migrations/tests.rs, src/db/migrations/v12.rs, src/db/migrations/v13.rs, src/db/migrations/v14.rs, src/db/migrations/v15.rs, src/db/migrations/v16.rs. The vN.rs files each have a `setup_migrated_db()` local helper — DELETE these locals and replace calls with the new shared helper. For v13.rs:49-55 (and analogous in other vN files), the pattern uses `run_migrations`, so `setup_test_db()` is the correct replacement. For tests.rs, look for tests that build up from a clean state with explicit `migrate_up(&mut conn).unwrap();` calls — those need `setup_test_db_unmigrated()`. Risk R3 from the PRD applies here — be deliberate.

correctness: NO test silently switches from 'partial migration' to 'full migration' — preserve the test author's intent
Test count unchanged per file
Schema version assertions in migration tests still hold (these tests typically check `get_schema_version(&conn) == 13` etc.)
style: Local `setup_migrated_db` helpers in vN.rs files are DELETED (replaced by the shared helper)
Imports cleaned

userStories.title: Review test-fixture refactor for quality, wiring, and lurking issues
userStories.description: Review all migrated files for: (1) borrow-checker correctness on `&db.conn` / `&mut db.conn` rebinding, (2) imports cleaned (no orphans), (3) preserved test count, (4) preserved test author intent (especially in migration tests), (5) helper actually exists and is exported correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `grep -U -rn 'TempDir::new\(\).*\n.*open_connection.*\n.*create_schema' src/ --include='*.rs'` — must return ZERO hits outside src/db/test_utils.rs
Run `grep -rn 'fn setup_db\(\)\|fn setup_test_db\(\)' src/ --include='*.rs'` — must return ONLY the canonical declarations in src/db/test_utils.rs
Run `cargo test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-results.txt | tail -3 && grep 'FAILED\|error\[' /tmp/test-results.txt | head -10` — exit code 0, no failures
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy.txt | tail -3 && grep '^error' /tmp/clippy.txt | head -10` — exit code 0, no warnings
Run `cargo fmt --check` — exit code 0
Confirm `src/learnings/test_helpers.rs` still exports `retire_learning` and `insert_task_with_files` (grep)

Confirm `src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs` still exports `EnvGuard`, `CLAUDE_BINARY_MUTEX`, `insert_test_learning`, `setup_git_repo`, `get_task_status`, `insert_task`, `insert_relationship`, `insert_prd_metadata`, `insert_done_task` (grep)
Any issues found have corresponding CODE-FIX-xxx or WIRE-FIX-xxx tasks added to the JSON with priority 14-16 and added to MILESTONE-1 dependsOn
userStories.notes: Pure refactor → focus on completeness and integration wiring. Use the rust-python-code-reviewer subagent if available. The most likely failure modes are: (a) a file has TWO independent test functions, only one was migrated; (b) borrow checker panics in a transaction-using site; (c) a migration test silently lost stepwise coverage by switching to setup_test_db (per Risk R3). For each issue: add a CODE-FIX-xxx (priority 14-16), add it to MILESTONE-1 dependsOn, commit JSON. Mark passes:true once all issues have spawned tasks (or 'No issues found' note).

correctness: Every grep listed in acceptanceCriteria is actually run and reported in progress
Every preserved-symbol verification is actually grep'd
style: n/a — review only

userStories.description: Trace the data/control path: src/db/test_utils.rs → consumers across src/commands/, src/loop_engine/, src/db/, src/learnings/. Confirm imports resolve, helper is callable, and the #[cfg(test)] gating actually keeps it out of release builds.
acceptanceCriteria: Trace path: `src/db/mod.rs` declares `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate) mod test_utils;` → `crate::db::test_utils::setup_test_db` is importable from every other module under `src/`
Run `cargo test --lib 2>&1 | tee /tmp/int.txt | tail -5 && grep 'test result' /tmp/int.txt` — total test count equals the count BEFORE this refactor began (record both numbers in the progress file)
Run `cargo build --release 2>&1 | tee /tmp/release.txt | tail -3` — succeeds with no warnings
CONTRACT: `let db = setup_test_db(); &db.conn` returns a `&rusqlite::Connection` (verify by compiling a one-line snippet in the test_utils unit-test mod)
CONTRACT: `db.db_dir()` returns `&std::path::Path` (compile-time check)
No dead code along the traced path — `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean
userStories.notes: Pure verification — no new code unless wiring is missing. The 'baseline test count' should be captured BEFORE FEAT-001 starts (record it during TEST-INIT-001 progress note). If baseline wasn't captured, use `git stash` + `cargo test` on a clean main as a fallback.

correctness: Test count parity is the gate; do not skip it
performance: Release build time within ±5% of baseline
style: n/a

userStories.title: Final refactoring review for the entire P1 scope

acceptanceCriteria: All implementation and test files reviewed
No dead imports, no leftover `setup_db()` references in comments, no commented-out 4-line blocks
Test author intent preserved across all migration tests
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-3-xxx tasks added to JSON with priority 71-85 and added to MILESTONE-FINAL dependsOn

userStories.title: Final verification: full test suite, clippy, fmt, grep sweeps, doc updates
userStories.description: Run the full quality gate one more time. Update CLAUDE.md with a brief 'Testing Database Setup' note pointing at src/db/test_utils.rs.
acceptanceCriteria: `cargo test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-results.txt | tail -3 && grep 'FAILED\|error\[' /tmp/test-results.txt | head -10` — exit 0, no failures
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy.txt | tail -3 && grep '^error' /tmp/clippy.txt | head -10` — exit 0, no warnings
`cargo fmt --check` — exit 0
`rg -U 'TempDir::new\(\).*\n.*open_connection.*\n.*create_schema' src/ --type rust` returns ONLY src/db/test_utils.rs
`rg 'fn setup_db\(\)|fn setup_test_db\(\)' src/ --type rust` returns ONLY src/db/test_utils.rs
`cargo build --release` succeeds with no warnings; release binary contains no 'test_utils' symbols (best-effort grep on `nm`/`strings` output)
CLAUDE.md (project-level) updated with a short 'Testing Database Setup' section linking src/db/test_utils.rs and explaining the two helpers (3-5 lines, not a full doc page)
`docs/` directory NOT updated — this is test plumbing, not architecture (skip unless something architectural changed)
All new public functions reachable from at least one entry point (test modules count)
No `.unwrap()` in production code paths (test setup is allowed to use `.expect()`)
userStories.notes: Final gate. The grep sweeps are the most important — they prove the duplication is gone. CLAUDE.md update should be terse: 'Tests use `crate::db::test_utils::setup_test_db()` to get a fully-migrated TestDb (or `setup_test_db_unmigrated()` for stepwise migration tests).' That's it.

correctness: Every grep listed in acceptanceCriteria is actually run and reported
performance: Final test suite runtime within ±10% of baseline
style: CLAUDE.md note is terse and accurate
